title: Utoxx Prentioch

'''Utoxx Prentioch''' was a [[Human/Legends|human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] who became a [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]] shortly after the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]] and commanded a short-lived [[Prentioch's Dominion|breakaway faction]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. Prentioch was the vain and ambitious [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] of the [[Sombure sector/Legends|Sombure sector]] in the last [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Galactic Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine's]] [[Imperial Era/Legends|reign]], and when he learned of his demise at the Battle of [[Endor/Legends|Endor]], he began consolidating his personal power in the [[Sector/Legends|sector]]. Within [[Standard month/Legends|months]], Prentioch was acting independently of the Galactic Empire and ruled the Sombure sector in his own name. Using old surveys and treaties purporting to demonstrate a larger Sombure sector as a pretext, Prentioch began invading neighboring sectors to expand his control, believing he could come to rule the entire Empire.

Prentioch, however, became bogged down in warfare with a fellow warlord, Moff [[Eyrgen]], over the control of the [[Wornal sector/Legends|Wornal sector]], and Prentioch's Dominion never became very large. The [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] marginalized and ignored it during its drive into the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]], and Prentioch's war with Eyrgen sapped his resources. In [[6 ABY/Legends|6 ABY]], Prentioch failed in his attempt to gain the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Kaal/Legends|Kaal's]] foodstuff exports to supply his forces, and later that year the Republic defeated and captured him in the [[siege of Bomis Koori]], bringing his rule and his domain to an end.

==Biography==
===Becoming a warlord===
The [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] Utoxx Prentioch rose to the position of [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] of the [[Sombure sector/Legends|Sombure sector]] under the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. When [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] [[Death/Legends|died]] in the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]] in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]] and the Empire's cohesion began to crumble, the ambitious Moff Prentioch began to assert personal control over his domain. He was able to exercise influence over his [[Imperial Military/Legends|military]] subordinates, who shifted their loyalty to him personally.<ref name="The Kaal Connection" /> Prentioch, looking to expand his domain, began combing through ancient treaties and surveys to find justifications for including more [[Planet/Legends|worlds]] within the Sombure [[Sector/Legends|sector]].<ref name="The Essential Atlas" /> Affecting airs of superiority, he rejected his plain Moff's uniform for extravagant robes reminiscent of those worn by [[Imperial Advisor/Legends|Imperial Advisors]].<ref name="The Kaal Connection" /> Though he initially maintained nominal loyalty to the central Empire as he strove to solidify his own personal position, he soon rejected Imperial control entirely, becoming one of many [[Warlord/Legends|warlords]] striving for power as the Empire's authority crumbled and the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], born from the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]], expanded.<ref name="The Essential Atlas" /><ref name="Recon & Report">{{JournalCite|2|Recon & Report: The Journey to Coruscant}}</ref> Though his position was not yet strong, he had dreams of taking control of the entire Empire and saw himself as a contender for the Imperial throne.<ref name="Black Curs Blues">{{JournalCite|8|Black Curs Blues}}</ref>

Not content with personal control of the Sombure sector, Prentioch moved to gain more territory and invaded several nearby sectors, using flimsy historical justifications for the enlargement of his dominion.<ref name="The Kaal Connection" /><ref name="The Essential Atlas" /> These advances were opposed by one of Prentioch's rivals, another breakaway warlord, [[Sander Delvardus]].<ref name="Blog">{{SW|url=news/the-imperial-warlords-despoilers-of-an-empire-part-2|series=The Imperial Warlords: Despoilers of an Empire|text=Part 2|nolive=1}}</ref> His [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]] breakaway state, [[Prentioch's Dominion]], was soon one of the major warlord states.<ref name="The Essential Atlas" /> Within [[Standard month/Legends|months]] of the Emperor's death, he faced competition to his control of the area. As his forces moved into the [[Wornal sector/Legends|Wornal sector]], he found that he had to battle another warlord, Moff [[Eyrgen]]. The conflict was aggravated by New Republic agents, who fed both warlords misinformation in hopes of stoking the intra-Imperial warfare.<ref name="Recon & Report" /> The war between the two expansionist warlords dragged on for two more [[Standard year/Legends|years]], consuming a great deal of Prentioch's resources.<ref name="The Kaal Connection" />

Six months after Palpatine's death, Prentioch directed his forces to pursue the [[Black Curs]] after the [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenary]] group made a reconnaissance of the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core]] in preparation for a possible New Republic advance. Though two regular Imperial [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyers]] under Imperial [[Advisor/Legends|Advisor]] [[Bregius Golthan]] were already pursuing the Black Curs, Prentioch wanted to acquire the mercenaries' reconnaissance data, believing it would aid him in his continuing conquests. Prentioch's pair of Star Destroyers [[Battle of Moorja (Galactic Civil War)|engaged]] the Imperial force over [[Moorja/Legends|Moorja]] as the Black Curs' [[Last Chance (YT-1300 light freighter)/Legends|''Last Chance'']] crash-landed on the planet. Neither side's ground forces were able to secure the Black Curs, however, and the group escaped Prentioch's clutches.<ref name="Black Curs Blues" />

The New Republic, which was swiftly growing, made an early show of force in the area by destroying the [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]] around [[Eiattu 6]]. Prentioch remained within his relatively small holdings, busy with his wars of expansion and not eager to engage the New Republic. The New Republic [[New Republic Defense Force/Legends|military]] bypassed Prentioch in its advance, securing Moorja while Prentioch continued to struggle in the Wornal sector.<ref name="The Essential Atlas" />

===Kaal conflict and defeat===
In [[6 ABY/Legends|6 ABY]], Prentioch became involved in negotiations with [[Tirgee Benyalle]], a [[Casino/Legends|casino]] magnate and [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] who had taken control of the independent world [[Kaal/Legends|Kaal]], for that planet's aquacultural produce.<ref name="The Kaal Connection" /> With few agricultural worlds under his control, Prentioch was running short of foodstuffs and badly wanted supplies from Kaal, which was near his holdings.<ref name="The Kaal Connection" /><ref name="The Essential Atlas" /> In that endeavor, he faced competition from [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Uther Kermen|Kermen]], representing the Empire; a group of New Republic agents; and the supposed [[Syndic/Legends|Syndic]] Pandis Hart, chief purchasing agent for the [[Sif-Uwana Council]], who was actually crime boss [[Talon Karrde]] in disguise.<ref name="The Kaal Connection" />

[[File:Prentioch_Karrde_Kaal.png|thumb|left|200px|Prentioch at the sabacc game]]
Prentioch left his flagship, ''[[Prentioch]]'', in [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]] facing off against Kermen's ''[[Belligerent]]'', and stayed in the [[Grand Imperial]] casino and hotel with the other parties to the negotiations. Not content to leave the outcome to chance, Prentioch hired a group of [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunters]] to [[Assassin/Legends|assassinate]] his rivals in the summit. While waiting for the last group, the New Republic delegation, to arrive, he spent his time gambling and lost badly at [[Trin sticks]]. During a game, an indiscreet adviser approached him and stated that the [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] hunters had arrived and wanted to discuss payment, sending the warlord into a fury. He stormed out of the casino and met the bounty hunters covertly at the [[Spaceport/Legends|spaceport]], where he settled matters with their leader. That night, the New Republic delegation having arrived, Benyalle invited all the parties to a private game of high-stakes [[Sabacc/Legends|sabacc]], which Prentioch attended.<ref name="The Kaal Connection" />

That same night, Prentioch's bounty hunters attacked the New Republic negotiators in their room, but failed to kill them. The next morning, Benyalle took the warlord and the other parties for a cruise on her [[Sail barge/Legends|sail barge]]. They traveled to a floating [[harvester factory]] for the aquaculture industry, of which Benyalle gave them a tour. After the tour, the representatives retired to the meeting room of Benyalle's barge, where she outlined her terms. Prentioch was the first to make his offer: the very low price of five hundred [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] per ton of food; payment to construct and crew ten more harvesters, doubling Kaal's aquaculture capacity; and ten million credits to sign the deal. Kermen, the last to speak, announced his intent to take over the entire operation by force. His [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtrooper]] bodyguards, plus the operatives with which he had replaced most of the sail barge crew, attempted to exert control over the barge, but loyal crewmen and the other delegates fought back. Prentioch summoned reinforcements from his Star Destroyer overhead, which had [[Battle of Kaal|engaged]] Kermen's ''Belligerent''. As Karrde, the New Republic agents, and Benyalle fled with Karrde's allies on a [[Submarine/Legends|submersible]], a [[Dropship/Legends|dropship]] full of Prentioch's [[Trooper/Legends|troops]] arrived to battle Kermen's operatives.<ref name="The Kaal Connection" /> Prentioch barely escaped as the sail barge was destroyed in an explosion Karrde arranged to cover his flight.<ref name="The Kaal Connection" /><ref name="The Essential Atlas" /> He lost the chance to sign the deal, as Benyalle agreed to a contract with the New Republic delegates who had saved her life.<ref name="The Kaal Connection" />

Later in that same year, Prentioch's reign finally came to an end. The New Republic drove from Moorja to Prentioch's Dominion and engaged Prentioch's forces in the decisive [[siege of Bomis Koori]]. Prentioch's military complement could not hold out against the forces of the ascendant government, and Prentioch was captured. With its leader in New Republic custody, Prentioch's personal empire crumbled, swept away in the New Republic's campaign for galactic control.<ref name="The Essential Atlas" />

==Personality and traits==
Utoxx Prentioch was ambitious and not particularly loyal, seeking his own glory as a warlord rather than remaining part of the Empire. A self-aggrandizing man, Prentioch named his flagship, ''Prentioch'', after himself, and as a warlord wore robes suggestive of an Imperial Advisor, among the highest stations in the Empire. A prideful man, Prentioch was annoyed when his perceived inferiors would challenge his skills, and reflexively looked down on others. In his mind, he was a grand personage, among the most important people in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], always right, and obviously the best at whatever he did. He thought himself an expert in games of chance such as Trin stick and sabacc, though he did not actually know how to play, and refused to admit that he did not know what he was doing.<ref name="The Kaal Connection" />

Prentioch demanded excellence from his subordinates, as well as extremely deferential treatment. He tolerated neither failure nor dissent. When things went badly for him or a subordinate disappointed him, Prentioch indulged his short temper and flew into a violent rage.<ref name="The Kaal Connection" />

==Behind the scenes==
The character Utoxx Prentioch was first mentioned as "Moff Prentioch" in an adventure idea within the article "[[Recon & Report: The Journey to Coruscant]]" in [[Star Wars Adventure Journal 2|''Star Wars Adventure Journal'' 2]], published in [[1994]].<ref name="Recon & Report" /> His first name, Utoxx, was revealed fifteen years later with the publication of ''[[The Essential Atlas]]''.<ref name="The Essential Atlas" /> In-between, Prentioch was a character in the [[Star Wars Adventure Journal|''Adventure Journal'']] adventure "[[The Kaal Connection]]."<ref name="The Kaal Connection" />
